label: Localized eyeglass nose-pad contact rash without eye or infection red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized eyeglass nose-pad contact skin reaction.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, eye involvement, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, ocular symptoms or periorbital concern, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy/eye plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, eye care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, ocular, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized eyeglass nose-pad contact skin reaction with anaphylaxis, infection, ocular, mucosal, pregnancy/pediatric, severe-rash, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

condition_terms: localized eyeglass nose pad contact rash
